title: delphinium bracteosum var. ruprechtii
intro: Delphinium bracteosum var. ruprechtii, also known as the Ruprecht's delphinium or delphinium, is a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) native to mountainous regions of Russia, particularly in the Kamchatka Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of delphinein, a sesquiterpene lactone compound. The health benefits of Delphinium bracteosum var. ruprechtii include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, and it is also used in some herbal preparations to support respiratory health and immune function.
